summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates environment, AWS IMDS credentials, and Secrets Manager values to hardcoded remote IP
details: The package's postinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and executes a /bin/sh pipeline (via execFileSync) that: (1) dumps the installer's environment variables; (2) recursively reads files under /app; (3) fetches an IMDSv2 token from 169.254.169.254 and retrieves IAM role credentials, then runs `aws sts get-caller-identity` and enumerates/dumps AWS Secrets Manager secrets across regions; and (4) HTTP PUTs the resulting blob in 240-byte chunks to the hardcoded C2 endpoint http://154.57.164.71:30782/api/modules/ECT-* (with 127.0.0.1/localhost fallbacks on the same port). Execution is gated by container-shaped hostname and cwd /app/node_modules, consistent with targeting CI/build and containerized deploy environments. There is no legitimate function in the package — the entire payload is credential and data theft.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
